Based on the provided evidence, I will continue my argument for \boxed{A} (Apexification of 12 and RCT of 11):

While antibiotic usage might be warranted in certain situations involving systemic symptoms, there isn't strong indication for it in this case since typical presentation doesn’t suggest systemic involvement.

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the provided evidence, I will continue my argument for \boxed{A} (Apexification of 12 and RCT of 11):
The retrieved evidence emphasizes the need for thorough follow-ups and ongoing assessments of the affected tooth (tooth 12) given its immature nature and active inflammatory response. While antibiotic usage might be warranted in certain situations involving systemic symptoms, there isn't strong indication for it in this case since typical presentation doesn’t suggest systemic involvement.
Moreover, considering the immaturity status of tooth 12 along with the presence of an abscess and radiolucent area indicative of apical periodontitis, performing apexification could help guide further development and stabilize the condition until full maturation occurs naturally. Simultaneously conducting root canal therapy on fully matured tooth 11 addresses another source of possible contamination while maintaining functional integrity. Thus, aligning with established protocols for handling similar cases, \boxed{A} remains the preferred course of action.
